features.evidence: now you do have windows hello to log you on which is great the camera is 5 megapixels 1080p so you do have a very good webcam and of course hp it has all these new software features that obviously let you change the exposure and do things auto frame sticker placement weird because it s an all amd sticker placement you think they d put more time and effort into placing these stickers a little bit straight but my feeling this was the last laptop they worked on that day a little drunk the guy he gets a little crazy around five o clock and just slapped it on and left his workplace now performance is very interesting because this is using a ryzen 7 5825u which is the first 5825 you have tested on this channel paired with 12 gigabytes of ram and a 512 gigabyte nvme ssd it beats out the i7 1260p with synthetic benchmarks but once you start doing other things creative work for example then the i7 1260p performs better because it utilizes that internal gpu now if you re a developer and all you need is strictly cpu power then this is superior it performed better in terms of my mozilla firefox compile test compared to some of the other user various processors from intel it s a solid cpu you just need to know what you re getting into if you re doing anything that s just regular office work or productivity then it really doesn t matter if you use intel or amd they re both going to perform equally well now this is obviously not a gaming laptop but if you don t mind dropping the settings quite a bit and playing some older titles the rx and vega 8 inside of here can handle some games nicely now fan noise on this is really good it never gets higher than 45 decibels when you have it on performance mode but if you don t want to hear the fans at all you got to drop it down to balance or quiet and it performs very very well heat management is also really good on this laptop even when this thing is running under full load the clock speeds stay nice and high whereas on intel cpus they have to drop down quite a bit in order to maintain proper cooling now internally i got some good news you could upgrade the ram it s not soldered onto the motherboard you could potentially put up to 32 gigabytes inside of here this happens to be rank 8 which is the good stuff there s two fans to cool the cpu which is a little bit overkill but my reasoning why they did this is because they offer an rtx 2050 version of this laptop and they probably use the same cooling design you have a swappable wi fi card you have your m2 slot right over here which you can upgrade for something bigger down the road and you have a 51 watt hour battery the good news though is you get really good battery life over 12 hours of use before needing to charge so look the hp envy xc60 is a pretty good laptop battery life is good performance is decent it s a solid product the only thing i wouldn t do is buy this specific sku because the display is absolutely awful aim for the oled version or even take a look at the 120 hertz ips version because it ll just get much better color accuracy in fact i d also keep your eye on the best buy website because i always see the envy on sale i wouldn t be surprised if you could pick up the oled version of this well under a thousand dollars there ll be links to that in the description down below if you have any more questions let me know the video if you liked it subscribe if you haven t already and i ll see you guys in the next one
value: average battery life in hours: 12 Hours

features.evidence: now you do have windows hello to log you on which is great the camera is 5 megapixels 1080p so you do have a very good webcam and of course hp it has all these new software features that obviously let you change the exposure and do things auto frame sticker placement weird because it s an all amd sticker placement you think they d put more time and effort into placing these stickers a little bit straight but my feeling this was the last laptop they worked on that day a little drunk the guy he gets a little crazy around five o clock and just slapped it on and left his workplace now performance is very interesting because this is using a ryzen 7 5825u which is the first 5825 you have tested on this channel paired with 12 gigabytes of ram and a 512 gigabyte nvme ssd it beats out the i7 1260p with synthetic benchmarks but once you start doing other things creative work for example then the i7 1260p performs better because it utilizes that internal gpu now if you re a developer and all you need is strictly cpu power then this is superior it performed better in terms of my mozilla firefox compile test compared to some of the other user various processors from intel it s a solid cpu you just need to know what you re getting into if you re doing anything that s just regular office work or productivity then it really doesn t matter if you use intel or amd they re both going to perform equally well now this is obviously not a gaming laptop but if you don t mind dropping the settings quite a bit and playing some older titles the rx and vega 8 inside of here can handle some games nicely now fan noise on this is really good it never gets higher than 45 decibels when you have it on performance mode but if you don t want to hear the fans at all you got to drop it down to balance or quiet and it performs very very well heat management is also really good on this laptop even when this thing is running under full load the clock speeds stay nice and high whereas on intel cpus they have to drop down quite a bit in order to maintain proper cooling now internally i got some good news you could upgrade the ram it s not soldered onto the motherboard you could potentially put up to 32 gigabytes inside of here this happens to be rank 8 which is the good stuff there s two fans to cool the cpu which is a little bit overkill but my reasoning why they did this is because they offer an rtx 2050 version of this laptop and they probably use the same cooling design you have a swappable wi fi card you have your m2 slot right over here which you can upgrade for something bigger down the road and you have a 51 watt hour battery the good news though is you get really good battery life over 12 hours of use before needing to charge so look the hp envy xc60 is a pretty good laptop battery life is good performance is decent it s a solid product the only thing i wouldn t do is buy this specific sku because the display is absolutely awful aim for the oled version or even take a look at the 120 hertz ips version because it ll just get much better color accuracy in fact i d also keep your eye on the best buy website because i always see the envy on sale i wouldn t be surprised if you could pick up the oled version of this well under a thousand dollars there ll be links to that in the description down below if you have any more questions let me know the video if you liked it subscribe if you haven t already and i ll see you guys in the next one

now you do have windows hello to log you on which is great the camera is 5 megapixels 1080p so you do have a very good webcam and of course hp it has all these new software features that obviously let you change the exposure and do things auto frame sticker placement weird because it s an all amd sticker placement you think they d put more time and effort into placing these stickers a little bit straight but my feeling this was the last laptop they worked on that day a little drunk the guy he gets a little crazy around five o clock and just slapped it on and left his workplace now performance is very interesting because this is using a ryzen 7 5825u which is the first 5825 you have tested on this channel paired with 12 gigabytes of ram and a 512 gigabyte nvme ssd it beats out the i7 1260p with synthetic benchmarks but once you start doing other things creative work for example then the i7 1260p performs better because it utilizes that internal gpu now if you re a developer and all you need is strictly cpu power then this is superior it performed better in terms of my mozilla firefox compile test compared to some of the other user various processors from intel it s a solid cpu you just need to know what you re getting into if you re doing anything that s just regular office work or productivity then it really doesn t matter if you use intel or amd they re both going to perform equally well now this is obviously not a gaming laptop but if you don t mind dropping the settings quite a bit and playing some older titles the rx and vega 8 inside of here can handle some games nicely now fan noise on this is really good it never gets higher than 45 decibels when you have it on performance mode but if you don t want to hear the fans at all you got to drop it down to balance or quiet and it performs very very well heat management is also really good on this laptop even when this thing is running under full load the clock speeds stay nice and high whereas on intel cpus they have to drop down quite a bit in order to maintain proper cooling now internally i got some good news you could upgrade the ram it s not soldered onto the motherboard you could potentially put up to 32 gigabytes inside of here this happens to be rank 8 which is the good stuff there s two fans to cool the cpu which is a little bit overkill but my reasoning why they did this is because they offer an rtx 2050 version of this laptop and they probably use the same cooling design you have a swappable wi fi card you have your m2 slot right over here which you can upgrade for something bigger down the road and you have a 51 watt hour battery the good news though is you get really good battery life over 12 hours of use before needing to charge so look the hp envy xc60 is a pretty good laptop battery life is good performance is decent it s a solid product the only thing i wouldn t do is buy this specific sku because the display is absolutely awful aim for the oled version or even take a look at the 120 hertz ips version because it ll just get much better color accuracy in fact i d also keep your eye on the best buy website because i always see the envy on sale i wouldn t be surprised if you could pick up the oled version of this well under a thousand dollars there ll be links to that in the description down below if you have any more questions let me know the video if you liked it subscribe if you haven t already and i ll see you guys in the next one

16 pounds so it s fairly light for a 15 inch laptop easy to carry around it s using a magnesium alloy there s a lot of ocean bound plastics wrapped into this as well top of the lid is quite squishy obviously because this is a darker color it s going to pick up fingerprints a lot easier and the reason why it s so squishy is because hp just their omen lineup leaves a gap right here so a gap in the center which means a lot of dust will collect and touch the keyboard but it tapers it at the end again i say this every single time a lot of people think this will allow the laptop to break easier but quite frankly i think it s better for thermal management you have have a lot of ports with a usb a port on the left hand side you have an audio jack a full size sd card slot which i really to see and then on the other side you have a usb a port hdmi and then two usb type c ports obviously these are not thunderbolt four because this is using an amd processor now unfortunately you can t open up this laptop with one hand this is a two hand device for the people that care about that but the screen of course goes 360 degrees now the hinge is going to be a bit looser than your traditional clamshell just because the screen has to rotate all the way around so keep that in mind it s pretty good for a two in one there s a bit of wobble but i ve used clamshell laptops that have more wobble than this so they did a pretty good job with the hinge the keyboard is really comfortable to type on i love the direction hp is going with their keyboards it s a big improvement from a few years ago and there s a lot of nice clickiness to the keys it feels super there s no numeric keypad obviously and it s a little bit soft in the middle there s no speakers on the bottom the speakers are on the top so you do have forward facing speakers they re okay they re not either obviously been equalized by big and olufsen but i don t think they re nearly as good as the macbook pro you have a big touch pad in the middle it s okay not the most accurate touch pad but more than good enough for for daily use and of course the keyboard does have backlighting in case you need it now my biggest complaint about this laptop is the display this is hands down one of the worst displays i ve looked at in the past month this displays color accuracy is really poor the color gambit is really bad and it s unexcusable for a laptop at this price point if this was a 500 600 laptop i might give them a pass but not on a thousand dollar laptop the weird thing too is that for an extra 100 bucks you can get an oled display with four more gigabytes of ram bringing it up to 16 from 12

quite frankly i think it s better for thermal management you have have a lot of ports with a usb a port on the left hand side you have an audio jack a full size sd card slot which i really to see and then on the other side you have a usb a port hdmi and then two usb type c ports obviously these are not thunderbolt four because this is using an amd processor now unfortunately you can t open up this laptop with one hand this is a two hand device for the people that care about that but the screen of course goes 360 degrees now the hinge is going to be a bit looser than your traditional clamshell review_summary: now you do have windows hello to log you on which is great the camera is 5 megapixels 1080p so you do have a very good webcam and of course hp it has all these new software features that obviously let you change the exposure and do things auto frame sticker placement weird because it s an all amd sticker placement you think they d put more time and effort into placing these stickers a little bit straight but my feeling this was the last laptop they worked on that day a little drunk the guy he gets a little crazy around five o clock and just slapped it on and left his workplace now performance is very interesting because this is using a ryzen 7 5825u which is the first 5825 you have tested on this channel paired with 12 gigabytes of ram and a 512 gigabyte nvme ssd it beats out the i7 1260p with synthetic benchmarks but once you start doing other things creative work for example then the i7 1260p performs better because it utilizes that internal gpu now if you re a developer and all you need is strictly cpu power then this is superior it performed better in terms of my mozilla firefox compile test compared to some of the other user various processors from intel it s a solid cpu you just need to know what you re getting into if you re doing anything that s just regular office work or productivity then it really doesn t matter if you use intel or amd they re both going to perform equally well now this is obviously not a gaming laptop but if you don t mind dropping the settings quite a bit and playing some older titles the rx and vega 8 inside of here can handle some games nicely now fan noise on this is really good it never gets higher than 45 decibels when you have it on performance mode but if you don t want to hear the fans at all you got to drop it down to balance or quiet and it performs very very well heat management is also really good on this laptop even when this thing is running under full load the clock speeds stay nice and high whereas on intel cpus they have to drop down quite a bit in order to maintain proper cooling now internally i got some good news you could upgrade the ram it s not soldered onto the motherboard you could potentially put up to 32 gigabytes inside of here this happens to be rank 8 which is the good stuff there s two fans to cool the cpu which is a little bit overkill but my reasoning why they did this is because they offer an rtx 2050 version of this laptop and they probably use the same cooling design you have a swappable wi fi card you have your m2 slot right over here which you can upgrade for something bigger down the road and you have a 51 watt hour battery the good news though is you get really good battery life over 12 hours of use before needing to charge so look the hp envy xc60 is a pretty good laptop battery life is good performance is decent it s a solid product the only thing i wouldn t do is buy this specific sku because the display is absolutely awful aim for the oled version or even take a look at the 120 hertz ips version because it ll just get much better color accuracy in fact i d also keep your eye on the best buy website because i always see the envy on sale i wouldn t be surprised if you could pick up the oled version of this well under a thousand dollars there ll be links to that in the description down below if you have any more questions let me know the video if you liked it subscribe if you haven t already and i ll see you guys in the next one now you do have windows hello to log you on which is great the camera is 5 megapixels 1080p so you do have a very good webcam and of course hp it has all these new software features that obviously let you change the exposure and do things auto frame sticker placement weird because it s an all amd sticker placement you think they d put more time and effort into placing these stickers a little bit straight but my feeling this was the last laptop they worked on that day a little drunk the guy he gets a little crazy around five o clock and just slapped it on and left his workplace now performance is very interesting because this is using a ryzen 7 5825u which is the first 5825 you have tested on this channel paired with 12 gigabytes of ram and a 512 gigabyte nvme ssd it beats out the i7 1260p with synthetic benchmarks but once you start doing other things creative work for example then the i7 1260p performs better because it utilizes that internal gpu now if you re a developer and all you need is strictly cpu power then this is superior it performed better in terms of my mozilla firefox compile test compared to some of the other user various processors from intel it s a solid cpu you just need to know what you re getting into if you re doing anything that s just regular office work or productivity then it really doesn t matter if you use intel or amd they re both going to perform equally well now this is obviously not a gaming laptop but if you don t mind dropping the settings quite a bit and playing some older titles the rx and vega 8 inside of here can handle some games nicely now fan noise on this is really good it never gets higher than 45 decibels when you have it on performance mode but if you don t want to hear the fans at all you got to drop it down to balance or quiet and it performs very very well heat management is also really good on this laptop even when this thing is running under full load the clock speeds stay nice and high whereas on intel cpus they have to drop down quite a bit in order to maintain proper cooling now internally i got some good news you could upgrade the ram it s not soldered onto the motherboard you could potentially put up to 32 gigabytes inside of here this happens to be rank 8 which is the good stuff there s two fans to cool the cpu which is a little bit overkill but my reasoning why they did this is because they offer an rtx 2050 version of this laptop and they probably use the same cooling design you have a swappable wi fi card you have your m2 slot right over here which you can upgrade for something bigger down the road and you have a 51 watt hour battery the good news though is you get really good battery life over 12 hours of use before needing to charge so look the hp envy xc60 is a pretty good laptop battery life is good performance is decent it s a solid product the only thing i wouldn t do is buy this specific sku because the display is absolutely awful aim for the oled version or even take a look at the 120 hertz ips version because it ll just get much better color accuracy in fact i d also keep your eye on the best buy website because i always see the envy on sale i wouldn t be surprised if you could pick up the oled version of this well under a thousand dollars there ll be links to that in the description down below if you have any more questions let me know the video if you liked it subscribe if you haven t already and i ll see you guys in the next
